message:
feat(core,abi): port /etc/rpc parser to core::rpc (bd-rpc-epic)

Lifts the /etc/rpc line parsing logic out of unistd_abi.rs (which had
it duplicated three times — once in parse_rpc_line_to_static and once
each inline in getrpcbyname's name+alias filter loop and
getrpcbynumber's number filter loop) into a pure-safe
frankenlibc_core::rpc module.

New core surface:
  pub struct RpcEntry { name: Vec<u8>, number: i32, aliases: Vec<Vec<u8>> }
  pub fn parse_rpc_line(line: &[u8]) -> Option<RpcEntry>
  pub fn lookup_rpc_by_name(content, name) -> Option<RpcEntry>
                                              (alias-aware, case-insens)
  pub fn lookup_rpc_by_number(content, num) -> Option<RpcEntry>

abi changes:
  - parse_rpc_line_to_static now does: core::rpc::parse_rpc_line ->
    fill_rpc_tls_from_entry(entry). The TLS storage (RPC_BUF /
    RPC_ENT / RPC_ALIASES) and the layout-stable rpcent struct
    writes (r_name@0, r_aliases@8, r_number@16) stay at the boundary.
  - getrpcbyname / getrpcbynumber drop their inline filter loops and
    just read the file + call core::rpc::lookup_*. Name matching
    becomes consistently alias-aware AND case-insensitive (the old
    inline impl was alias-aware but case-sensitive; the new core
    helper folds case for both name and aliases).

Verified:
  - 17 new core::rpc unit tests pass
  - 1/1 rpc_reentrant_wrappers_match_host_shapes abi test passes
  - 173/173 abi lib tests pass

Closes bd-rpc-epic.
